title: Layout Primitives
intro: Vertical and Horizontal are Excalibrr's flexbox primitives — every page scaffold, split pane, and toolbar is composed from them, with all geometry set through props. DashboardWidget and WidgetHeader frame content tiles; Overlay gates a region behind an antd Spin while it loads.

blocks.title: When to reach for these
blocks.body:
Use `Vertical` and `Horizontal` anywhere you would otherwise write `<div style={{ display: 'flex' }}>` — page scaffolds, split panes, control bars, button rows, form footers. Each renders a single flex `div` whose geometry is set entirely by props (`flex`, `gap`, `width`, `height`, `justifyContent`, `alignItems`), which keeps layout declarative and reviewable. Passing the same values through `style` is the #1 reviewed-out mistake in generated code.

The two are not symmetric. `Vertical` is greedy: it defaults to `flex: '1 1 auto'` and `height: '100%'`, so it fills whatever its parent gives it. `Horizontal` hugs content: it defaults to `flex: '0 1 auto'` with no height. Both set `overflow: hidden` unless you pass `scroll` — pick the one pane that scrolls and mark it explicitly.

blocks.title: Horizontal
blocks.caption: Horizontal states: default row hugging its children; justifyContent="space-between" splitting a full-width row; alignItems="center" cross-axis alignment with mixed-height children; gap={16} spacing. The "via style" card label is a showcase typo — the code behind it passes gap={16} as a prop.

blocks.title: Vertical
blocks.caption: Vertical states: "Default" stack (children stretch full-width) and flex="1" claiming all available space inside a fixed-height flex parent. The "Default" card is not pure default — the showcase overrides with style={{ height: 'auto' }} so the card hugs content; a true default Vertical fills its parent via height '100%'.

blocks.title: Vertical / Horizontal props
blocks.intro: Both share one prop surface; defaults differ where noted. Every layout concern has a prop — `style` exists only as an escape hatch for properties with no prop equivalent.

rows.notes: Vertical grows to fill by default; Horizontal hugs content. Pass flex="1" to make a row claim remaining space. Never style={{ flex: 1 }}.
Spacing between children; a number is pixels (gap={12}). The sanctioned spacing mechanism — never style={{ gap: '12px' }} and never margin on children.
Explicit sizing as props: height="100%", width={320}. Horizontal also has fullHeight (boolean) which adds the h-100 utility class.
Main-axis and cross-axis alignment, passed as props: justifyContent="space-between", alignItems="center".

Screen-axis centering shorthands. verticalCenter centers along the screen's vertical axis — justifyContent in Vertical, alignItems in Horizontal. horizontalCenter mirrors. They are not flex-axis aliases.
true → overflow: auto. false → overflow: hidden — overflowing content is clipped, not scrolled. Mark exactly one pane per region as the scroller.
Tonal background resolved from theme tokens — theme-safe in light and dark. Prefer this over background. See the bg tones table.
Renders var(--<value>): background="bg-2" → var(--bg-2). A raw color like "#fff" produces invalid CSS and silently fails. Legacy — prefer bg.
1px solid on the named side(s). Color comes from the theme's colorBorder token — or the matching status border color when bg is a status tone.
Corner rounding, usually paired with bg + border to make a soft panel.
Escape hatch, spread last so it wins. Use only for properties with no prop equivalent (e.g. position, minWidth) — never for flex, gap, height, or alignment.

blocks.title: bg tones
blocks.intro: The bg prop is the real variant axis on both primitives — a fixed set of theme-resolved surface tones.

rows.name: default
rows.when: Card-on-page content surface; resolves to the antd colorBgContainer token.

rows.name: elevated
rows.when: Raised panels, drawers, control bars; resolves to var(--bg-2).

rows.name: layout
rows.when: Page chrome and recessed wells behind cards; resolves to var(--bg-3).

rows.name: primary / info / success / warning / error
rows.when: Status-tinted callout regions; resolves to the matching antd colorXxxBg token. Pair with border and the border color switches to the matching colorXxxBorder automatically.

blocks.title: Canonical page scaffold
blocks.code:
// All layout via props — never style
<Vertical height='100%' gap={12}>
  {/* Header row: hugs its content height */}
  <Horizontal justifyContent='space-between' verticalCenter gap={8}>
    <Texto category='h4'>Quote Book</Texto>
    <GraviButton theme1 buttonText='Publish Prices' onClick={onPublish} />
  </Horizontal>

  {/* Body row: flex='1' claims remaining height */}
  <Horizontal flex='1' gap={12}>
    <Vertical flex='2' scroll>
      {/* main content / grid */}
    </Vertical>
    <Vertical flex='1' bg='elevated' border='left'>
      {/* side panel */}
    </Vertical>
  </Horizontal>
</Vertical>
blocks.note: Vertical fills by default, so the inner panes need no height. The body Horizontal needs flex='1' explicitly — rows hug content unless told to grow. The main pane gets scroll; everything else clips.

blocks.title: DashboardWidget
blocks.caption: DashboardWidget with no widgetTitle — a bare wrapper around children, no header row. The showcase's title="Revenue Summary" annotation is wrong twice over: the prop is widgetTitle, and it was never passed here.

blocks.title: WidgetHeader
blocks.caption: WidgetHeader with title="Monthly Stats" and an empty controls slot. The flair is the WidgetUnderline SVG; the uppercase/gray title styling from the library source CSS does not ship in the current package, so the text renders plain.

blocks.title: DashboardWidget / WidgetHeader props
blocks.intro: DashboardWidget is a `driver-widget` wrapper that optionally renders a WidgetHeader above its children. WidgetHeader is also exported standalone (as is UnderlineHeader, the title-plus-flair on its own). The header is an antd Row split into two Cols.

rows.notes: DashboardWidget. Renders a WidgetHeader above children; omit it and the wrapper renders headerless. The prop is widgetTitle — title is silently ignored.
DashboardWidget. Right-side header controls, forwarded to WidgetHeader's controls slot.
WidgetHeader. title is required and gets the WidgetUnderline flair; controls fills the right Col.
antd 24-column split between title and controls — keep the sum at 24 or the header wraps.
Col align passthrough; 'right' pushes controls to the header's right edge.
White underline flair for headers on dark surfaces.

blocks.title: Overlay props
blocks.intro: Overlay wraps a region in an antd `Spin` while it loads. When `renderOverlay` is true the children render dimmed behind the spinner; when false they render bare inside a Fragment. Toggling `renderOverlay` swaps the root element between `Spin` and `Fragment`, so React remounts the children subtree — component state and scroll position do NOT survive the flip. Hoist any state that must outlive the loading flash above the Overlay. No demo specimen exists yet; the surface below is verified against package types.

rows.notes: Required. true wraps children in <Spin>; false renders them untouched.
Text under the spinner — Spin's tip, renamed.
Custom spinner element — Spin's indicator, renamed.
Declared in the type but only tip/indicator are forwarded in the current implementation — do not rely on size or delay reaching the Spin.

items.why: Layout goes through props — this is the #1 reviewed-out mistake in generated Excalibrr code. style is the escape hatch, not the API.

items.dont: <Horizontal style={{ gap: '12px' }}> or margins on children
items.why: gap is the sanctioned spacing mechanism; numbers are pixels.

items.do: Pass scroll on the one pane that scrolls
items.dont: Expect overflowing content to scroll on its own
items.why: Both primitives set overflow: hidden by default — unscrolled overflow is clipped silently.

items.do: <Horizontal flex="1"> when a row must fill remaining height
items.dont: Assume rows grow like columns do
items.why: Horizontal defaults to flex '0 1 auto' (hugs); Vertical defaults to '1 1 auto' (fills).
The prop is widgetTitle; title is silently dropped and the header never renders.

items.title: Layout props, never style
items.detail: flex="1" not style={{ flex: 1 }}; height="100%" not style={{ height: '100%' }}; gap={12} not style={{ gap: '12px' }}; justifyContent="space-between" as a prop. style technically wins (it is spread last) which is exactly why it slips through review — hold the line.

items.title: Both primitives clip by default
items.detail: overflow is hidden unless scroll is passed. Content that outgrows its pane disappears with no scrollbar and no error — if something is missing at the bottom of a panel, check for a missing scroll first.

items.title: Vertical fills, Horizontal hugs
items.detail: Vertical defaults to flex '1 1 auto' + height '100%'; Horizontal defaults to flex '0 1 auto' with no height. Stacked Verticals split space evenly without any props; stacked Horizontals collapse to content height until one gets flex="1".

items.title: verticalCenter / horizontalCenter are screen-axis, not flex-axis
items.detail: verticalCenter means "center along the screen's vertical axis": it sets justifyContent in Vertical but alignItems in Horizontal. horizontalCenter mirrors. Read them as visual intent and they always do the right thing; read them as flex aliases and they look swapped.

items.title: background takes a CSS variable name, not a color
items.detail: background="bg-2" renders background: var(--bg-2). Passing "#fff" or "white" produces var(--#fff) — invalid CSS, silently no background. Prefer the bg tonal prop, which resolves real theme tokens in both light and dark.

items.title: DashboardWidget's flex prop is broken
items.detail: The class logic is inverted (flex ? '' : `flex-${flex}`): omitting flex emits a junk flex-undefined class, and passing it emits nothing. Size widgets with a parent Vertical/Horizontal and ignore this prop.

items.title: WidgetHeader spans are an antd 24-column split
items.detail: titleSpan and controlSpan default to 12/12 and must sum to 24 — a 16/12 split wraps the controls onto a second line.
